review_reason: F-preserving seed consistency conjecture is PROVABLY FALSE. Counterexample: for any F(psi') in w.world, setting phi = G(neg psi') makes the seed contain both G(neg psi') and neg(G(neg psi')), which is inconsistent. Priority 1 approach (F-preserving chain) is dead. User should choose: Priority 2 (Fragment-to-Int embedding, 20-35hrs) or Priority 3 (fragment-level completeness milestone, 5-10hrs).

artifacts.summary: Plan v7 blocked: F-preserving seed consistency conjecture proven false via counterexample

partial_progress.details: Phase 1 completed (analysis). Phase 2 blocked: counterexample found showing the F-preserving seed {phi} U FContent(w.world) U GContent(w.world) is inconsistent when phi = G(neg psi') for F(psi') in w.world. The seed contains complementary formulas G(neg psi') and F(psi') = neg(G(neg psi')).

errors.message: Conjecture is false: the F-preserving seed can contain {A, neg A} when the scheduled formula phi has the form G(neg psi') and F(psi') is in the fragment element's world.
errors.recommendation: Abandon F-preserving chain approach. Pursue Priority 2 (Fragment-to-Int embedding via orderIsoIntOfLinearSuccPredArch) or Priority 3 (accept fragment-level completeness as milestone).

completion_data.completion_summary: Analyzed F-preserving seed consistency conjecture from research-024. Discovered definitive counterexample proving the conjecture FALSE: when phi = G(neg psi') for any F(psi') in w.world, the seed contains complementary formulas. Plan v7 Priority 1 approach is dead. Recommends Priority 2 (embedding) or Priority 3 (fragment milestone).
